name: Webb, J. Lee
description: Collected some unknown insects in the summer of '98, one of which is now known to science as Tephritis webbii. Thesis: "Prison Labor." Degree, B. S. in Economic Science and History. Present line of work, Entomology. Subject of thesis for master's degree: "Insects Injurious to Forest Trees." Has described a new species of beetle, Cychrus idahoensis.

name: Cotton, John Storrs
clubs: First Regiment Infantry, New York Volunteers
description: Graduate of Union College, B. S. '97. Holding the college and New York state intercollegiate championships and records in the mile walk. Graduate of State Normal School, Oneonta, New York. Served with First Regiment Infantry, New York Volunteers during Spanish-American War. Taught school from April, '99, until February, '00. Now has charge of a grass experiment station near Sunnyside, Washington.
text: Graduate of Union College, B. S. '97. Holding the college and New York state intercollegiate championships and records in the mile walk. Graduate of State Normal School, Oneonta, New York. Now has charge of a grass experiment station near Sunnyside, Washington.

name: Carlisle, Charles Centennial
description: Foreigner. He migrated to Oakesdale in 1884. In 1892, after two years' attendance, he graduated from the public schools of Oakesdale. Desiring to see the world, he went to California, but after two years, returned. He has been prominent in military circles. Has been president of the Websterian for one semester this year. Thesis: "Water Measurements."
text: Foreigner. He migrated to Oakesdale in 1884. In 1892, after two years' attendance, he graduated from the public schools of Oakesdale. Desiring to see the world, he went to California, but after two years, returned. He has been prominent in military circles. Has been president of the Websterian for one semester this year. Thesis: "Water Measurements."

name: Mashburn, William Edward
description: The class president for two years—one by choice and one by inclination—is also a part of the foreign element. In company with the other members of his household he migrated to the United States in 1881. Entered the W. A. C. in the fall of '95 and took a business course, after which he discontinued his education for a year. In '97 he returned and entered the Electrical Engineering course. During his Sophomore year was president of the Columbian Society and the Glee Club. Thesis: "A Cross Compound Traction Engine for Burch's Boiler."

name: Mumm, Hans Jr.
clubs: United States Deputy Mineral Surveyor for Idaho
description: Foreigner number two. When two years old, he started toward America, never stopping until Rosalia was reached. Here Hans attended a little red country schoolhouse. When seventeen he entered the State Normal School at Cheney and remained a year and a half. Thesis: "Ratio Between the Rainfall and the Runoff in the Palouse Valley."

name: Nalder, Frank Fielding
clubs: Orator on Washington's birthday program for the Washington, '00
Member of intercollegiate debating team, in debate with the U. of W., '99
Won first prize in State Anti-Saloon League's Oratorical Contest at Seattle, '00, between Whitman, U. of W. and W.A.C.
description: The other foreigner. The first seven years of his life were spent in New Zealand, coming to America in 1883. He settled on a farm near Waitsburg, Washington. In his eighteenth year entered Waitsburg Academy. Thesis: "The Government Ownership of Railroads in Australia."
text: The other foreigner. The first seven years of his life were spent in New Zealand, coming to America in 1883. He settled on a farm near Waitsburg, Washington. In his eighteenth year entered Waitsburg Academy. Thesis: "The Government Ownership of Railroads in Australia."

name: Colburn, Grace Lula
clubs: Represented the class at Senior Prep. Graduation
Represented the Washington Society on Washington's birthday in '99
description: She is the youngest of the grave and reverend tribe. Grace went to "district school" near Deep Creek until some time in March, 1896, when she joined the Naughty-Ones. Her favorite color is lavender; her predominant characteristic, loyalty. She will submit a thesis on "The Indian in American Literature."
text: She is the youngest of the grave and reverend tribe. Grace went to "district school" near Deep Creek until some time in March, 1896, when she joined the Naughty-Ones. Her favorite color is lavender; her predominant characteristic, loyalty. She will submit a thesis on "The Indian in American Literature."

name: Beattie, Mabelle Benton
description: She went to public school just like the rest of the Seniors till she was thirteen, then moved with her parents to Bethany, a suburb of Lincoln, Nebraska. Studied at Cotner University and when sweet sixteen went to Peru, Nebraska. She stayed there till she graduated from the State Normal. Last year she studied at the State University at Lincoln, and to cap the climax of her schooling, she came to W.A.C.

name: Williams, Van Eaton
description: Attended grammar school and spent two years in the Tacoma High School where he reached the tenth grade. After some experience in an immigration office at Seattle, taught school one term, then joined the Northern Pacific Mineral survey in the spring of '97.
text: Attended grammar school and spent two years in the Tacoma High School where he reached the tenth grade. After some experience in an immigration office at Seattle, taught school one term, then joined the Northern Pacific Mineral survey in the spring of '97.

name: Outman, William Dell
clubs: Class President, Freshman and Sophomore years
Member, debating team which defeated Whitman
description: He received his early training in the public school. Between his seventeenth and nineteenth years taught several terms of school.
text: He received his early training in the public school. Between his seventeenth and nineteenth years taught several terms of school.

name: Lynch, Patrick J.
description: He says he received some early education in the country schools on the borders of the Yakima Indian Reservation. After spending three years in the State Normal at Ellensburg, entered the W. A. C. in the fall of '97, and enrolled in the Mining Engineering Course, believing that was the best course in school, and that there is nothing too good for the Irish. Last fall he gave the powers to understand that for his degree he would submit a thesis on "The Development of a Mine."

Last fall he gave the powers to understand that for his degree he would submit a thesis on "The Development of a Mine."

name: Goodsell, Charles H.
description: After a checkered earlier career, he came to Spokane, which has since been his home. Knows more tales about old times in the Dorm. than could be recorded in a year. Took a prominent part in the changing of the college colors. Thesis "The Development of a Silver-Lead Mine in the Coeur d'Alenes."
text: After a checkered earlier career, he came to Spokane, which has since been his home. Knows more tales about old times in the Dorm. than could be recorded in a year. Took a prominent part in the changing of the college colors. Thesis "The Development of a Silver-Lead Mine in the Coeur d'Alenes."

description: According to his own statement, began life on a farm. Attended the common schools and a seminary at Easton, New York. While yet seventeen, he began teaching school, and at nineteen years of age came to Washington. He taught one term, and went to California to recuperate. A year later he returned to Washington and entered the Prep. department of the W. A. C. He finished the Freshman year then, taught country school, returning to college in '98. Has done a good deal of tutoring. Thesis: "Design of Traction Engine Boiler and Running Gear."
